title: Dujmović–Joret–Morin–Norin–Wood Question on 2-tree-width and chromatic number
statement_text: There exists a function $f : \mathbb{N} \to \mathbb{N}$ such that every graph $G$ with 2-tree-width at most $k$ has chromatic number at most $f(k)$.
context_text: The authors of [5] asked whether $\chi(G)$ is bounded from above by a function of the 2-tree-width of $G$, motivated by the fact that this holds for 2-path-width (via the Asplund–Grünbaum theorem on rectangle intersection graphs). The present paper provides a negative answer, showing triangle-free Burling graphs with arbitrarily large chromatic number have 2-tree-width (and even mixed tree/path-decomposition width) at most 2.
attributed_to: Dujmović, Joret, Morin, Norin, and Wood
paper_contribution: Disproves the question by constructing, for every $k \geq 1$, a Burling graph $G_k$ with a tree-decomposition and a path-decomposition such that each bag of the first intersects each bag of the second in at most 2 vertices, while $\chi(G_k) \geq k$.
paper_title: Burling graphs, chromatic number, and orthogonal tree-decompositions

title: Conjecture 3
statement_text: There exists a function $f : \mathbb{N} \to \mathbb{N}$ such that $\chi(G) \leq f(k)$ for every $k \geq 1$ and every graph $G$ admitting a spaghetti tree-decomposition $(T, \{B_t\}_{t \in V(T)})$ and a path-decomposition $(P, \{B_p\}_{p \in V(P)})$ such that $|B_t \cap B_p| \leq k$ for every $t \in V(T)$ and $p \in V(P)$.
context_text: The authors conclude the paper with this conjecture as an open problem in the spirit of exploring how the Asplund–Grünbaum result could be extended. A spaghetti tree-decomposition is a tree-decomposition rooted at some vertex $r$ such that, orienting edges away from $r$, the subtree induced by bags containing any fixed vertex $v$ forms a directed path. The authors remark that the conjecture could even hold with two spaghetti tree-decompositions.
paper_title: Burling graphs, chromatic number, and orthogonal tree-decompositions
